content: MS treatment includes acute relapse management and disease-modifying therapies (DMTs). Acute relapse: high-dose IV methylprednisolone (1g/day × 3-5 days) or oral equivalent; plasma exchange for steroid-refractory cases. DMT selection based on disease activity, progression risk, and safety: (1) Injectable platforms (interferon-beta, glatiramer acetate)—modest efficacy, excellent safety, first-line for mild disease. (2) Oral agents: dimethyl fumarate (monitor lymphocytes, PML risk), teriflunomide (hepatotoxicity, teratogenic), fingolimod/siponimod/ozanimod (S1P modulators, bradycardia risk, macular edema, PML). (3) Infusions: natalizumab (high efficacy, PML risk—requires JC virus monitoring), ocrelizumab (anti-CD20, PPMS approved, infection risk), alemtuzumab (anti-CD52, highest efficacy but secondary autoimmunity risk), cladribine (immune reconstitution approach). Treatment strategy: escalation (start moderate, escalate if breakthrough) vs induction (start high-efficacy early for aggressive disease). Symptomatic management: baclofen/tizanidine for spasticity, gabapentin/pregabalin for neuropathic pain, amantadine/modafinil for fatigue, anticholinergics for bladder dysfunction.
